title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Automation and Data Client… · Behavior Case: Validators Conditional Requests
summary: A public-data mirror uses validators and local metadata but has confused stale files, 304 metadata, Vary, and authenticated responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py validators conditional requests increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers spec-aware cache decision notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
